title: No review-ready .docx export of the assembled whole-plan draft
description: The workshop assembles a whole-plan draft (prompt-workshop/outputs/current-draft.json) but can only render it as Markdown (sequence.py --render-whole-draft) and as ad-hoc static HTML; there is no export to a real Microsoft Word .docx for human review in the form reviewers actually use -- the canonical schoolwide-action-plan format exemplified by data/'Sample - Schoolwide Action Plan 2.docx' (a label/value header table + a 6-column action-step grid). python-docx is present in the .venv but is NOT declared in school-improvement-plans/pyproject.toml or uv.lock, so any exporter would depend on an undeclared package. No production-side draft->document export exists either. This is a delivery/review surface (orthogonal to the prompt-corpus work of FEAT-008) but is folded into the current focus per direction.

evidence.reference: the assembled whole-plan draft (shape defined in prompt-workshop/dispatch/_workshop.py:214-243); 13 top-level keys (plan, milestones, action_steps, success_criteria, feedback_channels, review_events, communications, revision_rules, accreditation_standards, domain_alignment, responsibilities, guiding_clauses, flags)
python-docx / beautifulsoup4 not declared (present in .venv only)
the target Word format: label/value header table + 6-column action grid

impact: Reviewers cannot get the generated draft in the Word form they review and sign in; the canonical action-plan .docx cannot be produced from a generated draft; this blocks human review/sign-off of workshop drafts and the eventual promotion sign-off surface for the orchestration trio (DEC-29).
proposed_resolution: Add a draft->docx exporter (prompt-workshop/dispatch/export_to_docx.py) that loads current-draft.json and emits a genuine OOXML .docx structured like the exemplar (header label/value table + bordered 6-col action grid), exporting any assembled draft (review surface; warn on missing sections, do not block); declare python-docx (and any HTML-parse dep) in the project dependency files. Folds into the current focus; eventually mirrored as a production export for the trio's review/sign-off.
